description: A small canonical matrix for exercising Riddle Proof as its own proving-ground product before scaling to broad generated user-story sweeps.
operating_rules: Keep each pass bounded: choose one batch, run it, classify findings, fix only high-signal issues, then rerun the same batch.
A story is not passed until it records concrete evidence receipts such as command output, profile-result.json, proof.json, screenshots, PR comment body, or a formal/test artifact.
Do not count GitHub Actions failures caused by billing or unavailable runners as product regressions. Classify those as environment_blocked.
Prefer generic Riddle Proof surfaces over adapter-specific wrapper behavior unless the story explicitly names an adapter.
Negative-control stories are first-class: a correct product_regression or proof_insufficient result can be a pass for the framework.

batch_policy.rerun_rule: After fixing a product or contract issue found by the batch, rerun every story in the same batch before advancing.
batch_policy.promotion_rule: Promote a story from candidate to recurring only after it has at least one saved evidence packet and a stable expected verdict.

stories.user_story: As a proof author, I can ask Riddle Proof to suggest checks for a changed route without accidentally treating suggestions as proof.
stories.expected_behavior: The command emits a draft profile or JSON suggestion and clearly does not produce a proof verdict.
evidence_required: generated profile includes route_loaded or selector checks
output contains no passed/product_regression/proof_insufficient verdict
operator records the saved draft path or stdout capture

stories.user_story: As a product maintainer, I can run a hosted profile against a live route and receive a passed verdict tied to screenshots and structured artifacts.
stories.expected_behavior: Hosted run-profile returns passed, writes profile-result.json, summary.md, proof.json, console.json, dom-summary.json, and records screenshot artifact names.
stories.command: riddle-proof-loop run-profile --profile <route-smoke-profile> --url https://riddledc.com --runner riddle --output artifacts/riddle-proof/<story-id> --result-format compact-json
evidence_required: profile-result.json status is passed
riddle.job_id is present
proof_json and screenshot artifacts are listed

stories.user_story: As a maintainer, I can run a negative-control profile and see a product_regression instead of a false pass.
stories.expected_behavior: A missing selector or missing query parameter produces product_regression with evidence, not passed and not a lifecycle crash.
stories.command: riddle-proof-loop regression-pack run --pack oc-flow-regression --local-core false --hosted-riddle true --format markdown --output artifacts/riddle-proof/hosted-regression
evidence_required: negative case expected status is product_regression
profile-result.json includes the failed check
summary names the failed invariant without hiding artifacts

stories.user_story: As a hosted runner user, I can submit work while workers are scaled to zero without the client declaring the job stale before normal cold-start readiness.
stories.expected_behavior: The unsubmitted retry window is long enough for accepted scale-to-zero cold starts, and progress output explains worker-wake waiting.
evidence_required: attempts budget corresponds to a five-minute unsubmitted timeout
progress output reports waiting_for_worker_submit before submission
profile-result.json passes or returns environment_blocked with the specific infra blocker

stories.user_story: As a framework maintainer, I can run the generic local core suite and prove route, query/hash, thrown-error, missing-selector, and no-diff cases without hosted infrastructure.
stories.expected_behavior: The local core regression suite passes all required cases and reports no forbidden terminal markers.
stories.command: riddle-proof-loop regression-pack run --pack oc-flow-regression --local-core true --hosted-riddle false --format compact-json --output artifacts/riddle-proof/local-core
evidence_required: regression-pack-result.json local_core.ok is true
missing_required_cases is empty
forbidden_terminal_markers_seen is empty

stories.user_story: As a reviewer, I can get a PR comment whose badge, artifact links, and failed checks reflect the saved proof packet rather than raw guessed fields.
stories.expected_behavior: Dry-run PR comment generation renders status and artifacts from proof outputs and refuses missing required inputs.
evidence_required: body file includes the same status as profile-result.json
required artifact links or local names are present
missing proof-dir inputs fail rather than rendering passed

stories.user_story: As a profile promoter, I can derive body assertions from a real artifact before making a public proof/profile claim.
stories.expected_behavior: The command emits snippets only when they are present in the artifact and fails when required fragments are missing.
evidence_required: required present fragment is emitted
required missing fragment exits nonzero
promoted profile records derived snippets instead of hand-authored guesses

stories.user_story: As a public proof publisher, I can check artifact URLs and body fragments before spending a hosted browser run.
stories.expected_behavior: The preflight returns success only when status, content type, byte size, required body text, and forbidden body text all satisfy the profile.
evidence_required: preflight output lists every checked URL
missing required body text fails
forbidden escaped/raw artifact text fails

stories.user_story: As a user whose local process was interrupted, I can recover a hosted job and still get the proof artifacts and summary.
stories.expected_behavior: Recover fetches the hosted job artifacts, writes the same profile-result/summary/proof files, and preserves the original job id.
evidence_required: recovered profile-result.json includes the requested job id
summary.md is written
proof.json and screenshots are recovered or explicitly reported missing

stories.user_story: As a maintainer running viewport matrices, I can aggregate child runs without losing the failing viewport or overstating total latency.
stories.expected_behavior: Aggregate returns the worst required child verdict, lists child outputs, and separates summed poll time from max child latency.
evidence_required: aggregate profile-result.json names all child viewports
any failed child remains visible in checks
summary reports child timing without implying a single hosted job

stories.user_story: As a framework maintainer, I can prove the result/status contract that runtime tests depend on, without putting Lean in the evidence-collection hot path.
stories.expected_behavior: The formal kernel builds, and conformance tests map the same runtime cases into executable checks.
stories.command: cd formal/riddle-proof-kernel && lake build && cd ../.. && node packages/riddle-proof/formal-conformance.test.js
evidence_required: lake build exits zero
formal-conformance.test.js exits zero
new runtime status cases are either mirrored in Lean or explicitly classified as out of formal scope

stories.user_story: As a Riddle Proof maintainer, I can run a non-Riddle static page through the local Playwright profile runner and receive the normal evidence packet.
stories.expected_behavior: The neutral pass profile returns passed, writes profile-result.json, proof.json, console.json, dom-summary.json, artifact-manifest.json, and screenshots.
evidence_required: profile-result.json status is passed
artifact-manifest.json records local artifacts
phone and desktop screenshots are written

stories.user_story: As a Riddle Proof maintainer, I can run a non-Riddle negative-control page profile and see product_regression instead of a false pass.
stories.expected_behavior: The neutral negative-control profile reaches the page, then fails only the deliberate missing selector and returns product_regression with evidence.
evidence_required: profile-result.json status is product_regression
failed check names [data-rp-fixture="missing-required-control"]
proof artifacts are still written for review

stories.user_story: As a hosted Riddle Proof user, I can run a non-Riddle static preview through hosted Riddle and receive a passed evidence packet.
stories.expected_behavior: The hosted neutral pass profile returns passed, includes the hosted job id, and lists proof JSON, console, DOM summary, and screenshots.
stories.command: riddle-proof-loop riddle-preview-deploy packages/riddle-proof/examples/neutral-fixture-site neutral-fixture-matrix --framework static && riddle-proof-loop run-profile --profile packages/riddle-proof/examples/profiles/neutral-fixture-pass.json --url <preview-url> --runner riddle --output artifacts/riddle-proof/neutral-fixture-pass-hosted --result-format compact-json
evidence_required: profile-result.json status is passed
riddle.job_id is present
hosted screenshot and JSON artifact URLs are listed

stories.user_story: As a hosted Riddle Proof user, I can run a non-Riddle negative-control static preview and see product_regression instead of a false pass.
stories.expected_behavior: The hosted neutral negative-control profile reaches the preview page, fails the deliberate missing selector, and returns product_regression with review artifacts.
evidence_required: profile-result.json status is product_regression
failed check names [data-rp-fixture="missing-required-control"]
hosted screenshot and JSON artifact URLs are listed

stories.user_story: As a Riddle Proof user, I can run a browser profile that supplies stored auth/session state before page checks execute.
stories.expected_behavior: The profile writes a deterministic localStorage token, reloads the page, reaches the authenticated fixture state, and produces normal proof artifacts locally and hosted.
evidence_required: profile-result.json status is passed
setup_actions_succeeded records local_storage with reload
hosted or local screenshots show the authenticated fixture state

stories.user_story: As a consumer of Riddle Proof summaries, I can evaluate passed, product_regression, proof_insufficient, environment_blocked, and checkpoint-audit packets without using Riddle docs as the target.
stories.expected_behavior: Neutral fixture packets generate hosted proof view and agent summary surfaces with the expected policy state, claims, disclosures, and suppressed stale merge recommendations.
evidence_required: neutral fixture packet set contains no Riddle Proof docs URLs
hosted proof view helper matches every expected fixture claim
agent summary helper matches every expected fixture claim

stories.user_story: As a host integration, I can ignore a stale checkpoint response after the run has already reached a terminal status.
stories.expected_behavior: A late response is recorded as ignored and does not reopen the run or start background resume.
stories.command: riddle-proof-loop regression-pack run --pack oc-flow-regression --local-core true --hosted-riddle false --format json --output artifacts/riddle-proof/checkpoint-stale
evidence_required: late-stale-checkpoint-ignored case is present
ignored_checkpoint_response is true
background_resume_started is false

stories.user_story: As a viewer of a hosted proof page, I see the contract-derived status, artifacts, and missing-evidence explanation rather than raw backend fields.
stories.expected_behavior: Hosted proof views and any agent-facing summaries import/use the same status/artifact contract as PR comments.
stories.command: Inspect hosted proof renderer imports and run a hosted proof view smoke with a passed and a negative-control packet.
evidence_required: renderer uses shared contract code or has a documented gap
passed packet renders passed with artifacts
negative-control packet renders product_regression or proof_insufficient, never passed
